skill.name: backend-fastapi
skill.description: FastAPI backend guide for chatbot and RAG endpoints, dependencies, and required service configuration.
skill.summary: Document FastAPI chatbot endpoints and backend dependencies.
skill.author: AbdulSamad94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, environment variable names, and one weak-cryptography pattern. Review found documentation text only: local run examples, endpoint names, file names, and required secret variable names, with no executable code, secret reading, network exfiltration, or prompt injection. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk from documented operational commands and secret configuration names.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. The cited lines are Markdown inline code for file paths, endpoint names, data type names, service names, and a local uvicorn run example; no executable Ruby, shell backtick syntax, or command interpolation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains prose and Markdown inline code, not Ruby or shell source. The only run command is a normal local development command and is not hidden or malicious.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Secret Variable Names Listed for Configuration
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged generic API or secret keys on lines 48 and 49. Those lines list required environment variable names for Gemini and Qdrant configuration; the skill does not read, print, transmit, or request secret values.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is limited to environment variable names in a documentation section. No code path accesses environment contents or sends them elsewhere.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3. Line 3 is the YAML description for FastAPI documentation and contains no hash algorithm, encryption primitive, or security implementation guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited line found only descriptive metadata. No cryptographic term or algorithm appears at that location.

content.value_statement: Teams need clear backend context before changing chatbot and RAG services. This skill summarizes FastAPI endpoints, dependencies, run commands, and service configuration in one focused guide.
actual_capabilities: Identifies the FastAPI application entry point and default local port.
Documents the main chat endpoint and its request and response flow.
Explains the selected-text question endpoint and validation step.
Lists backend utility modules for configuration, embeddings, prompts, and model setup.
Names required Gemini and Qdrant environment variables for service configuration.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not inspect the actual backend source files.
It does not create, modify, or test FastAPI routes.
It does not validate API keys, Qdrant connectivity, or model credentials.
It covers the documented backend structure only, not deployment or monitoring.

use_cases.title: Onboard Backend Contributors
use_cases.description: Help new engineers understand the FastAPI entry point, endpoint flow, and supporting modules before editing the service.

use_cases.title: Prepare API Documentation
use_cases.description: Turn the documented endpoint behavior into concise project notes for chatbot and selected-text question workflows.

use_cases.title: Review RAG Service Dependencies
use_cases.description: Check which services and environment variables are expected before running or troubleshooting the backend locally.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize the Backend
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-fastapi skill to summarize the FastAPI backend, its entry point, main endpoints, and required services.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Endpoint Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-fastapi skill to explain the request, processing steps, and response for /api/chat and /api/ask-selection.

prompt_templates.title: Create Onboarding Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-fastapi skill to create concise onboarding notes for a developer joining this chatbot backend project.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Dependency Assumptions
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-fastapi skill to identify backend dependency assumptions, required environment variables, and gaps to verify in source code.

output_examples.input: Summarize the backend architecture for a new contributor.
output_examples.output: A short architecture brief covering the FastAPI entry point, local run command, chat endpoint, selected-text endpoint, utility modules, and required services.

output_examples.input: Prepare endpoint notes for the RAG chat API.
output_examples.output: A readable endpoint summary that explains the query input, Qdrant search step, prompt building step, agent response generation, and returned contexts.

output_examples.input: List what must be configured before running the backend.
output_examples.output: A configuration checklist naming Gemini and Qdrant variables, the default local port, and the backend entry point to verify.

best_practices: Use this skill as a starting point, then verify details against the live backend source.
Keep API keys in environment variables or secret stores, never in prompts or documentation output.
Pair endpoint summaries with tests or OpenAPI output when preparing release documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not assume this documentation proves the backend currently runs.
Do not paste real Gemini or Qdrant secrets into prompts.
Do not use the listed local run command as a production deployment method.

faq.question: Does this skill generate FastAPI code?
faq.answer: No. It documents the backend structure, endpoints, dependencies, and configuration names already described in the skill file.

faq.question: Can it explain the main chat endpoint?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize the documented query input, Qdrant search, prompt building, agent response, and returned contexts.

faq.question: Does it require access to API keys?
faq.answer: No. It only names required environment variables and should not receive secret values.

faq.question: Can it validate the actual backend implementation?
faq.answer: No. It does not inspect source code beyond the documented SKILL.md content.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to use in a marketplace?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation-only content and no confirmed malicious behavior.
